remember: Students will be able to recall the definition of current, electric field and magnetic field due to a current-carrying conductor.
Students will be able to list down the factors on which the force between two parallel current-carrying conductors depends.
Students will be able to recall the conditions for the maximum force on a current-carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field.
Students will be able to list down the applications of moving charges and magnetism.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of current and its direction.
Students will be able to describe the electric field and magnetic field lines around a current-carrying conductor.
Students will be able to explain the force between two parallel current-carrying conductors and the conditions for its maximum value.
Students will be able to explain the working of a moving coil galvanometer.
apply: Students will be able to calculate the force on a current-carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field.
Students will be able to determine the direction of the force on a current-carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field.
Students will be able to design a simple moving coil galvanometer to measure current.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the dependence of the force between two parallel current-carrying conductors on the current, distance and magnetic permeability.
Students will be able to analyze the conditions for the maximum force on a current-carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field.

ncert_objectives: To define current and explain its direction.
To explain the concept of electric and magnetic fields due to a current-carrying conductor.
To study the force between two parallel current-carrying conductors and the conditions for its maximum value.
To study the properties of a moving coil galvanometer.
board_exam_objectives: To calculate the force on a current-carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field.
To determine the direction of the force on a current-carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field.
To explain the working of a moving coil galvanometer and its applications.
To analyze the dependence of the force between two parallel current-carrying conductors on the current, distance and magnetic permeability.
